title: ROCK VARNISH GEOCHEMICAL PROVENANCE ANALYSIS
what: XRD and XRF investigation of Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering in rock varnish — Late Holocene indigenous material sourcing in collaboration with Stony Brook University Anthropology and Brookhaven National Laboratory
Provenance analysis of steatite archaeological artifacts spanning Terminal Archaic through Late Woodland periods (3,500–2,400 B.P.)
how: X-ray Diffraction (XRD): 2θ = 20–75° sweeps for crystal structure identification and mineral phase characterization; d-spacing fingerprinting to establish unique crystallographic signatures for material sourcing
X-ray Fluorescence (XRF): elemental mapping of Fe, Mn, and trace elements across 10+ artifact samples cross-referenced against Rhode Island quarry reference sources
Comparative stratigraphic and elemental analysis across sample set spanning Terminal Archaic through Late Woodland periods
results: 6 samples with Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering consistent with Younger Dryas formation — constraining varnish age and deposition environment
10+ steatite artifacts traced to Rhode Island quarry sources; 10–45% elemental variation distinguishing regional clay sources and trade patterns
Confirmed long-distance trade networks (100+ miles); published in NYSAA Bulletin Vol. 138, 2024 and BNL technical report
